description:
You are working as a FinOps analyst who has just been asked to perform a very quick
“sanity-check” of the local network path that your cost-allocation micro-service
runs on.  
Management only wants a **single, three-line summary file** that they can ingest
into their monitoring dashboard.  
To build that file you will run two standard network-diagnostics commands and then
convert their results into the required summary.

1. Ping test  
   • Target host: 127.0.0.1  
   • Exactly 3 ICMP packets (`-c 3`)  
   • The summary we care about is the **packet-loss percentage** that appears in
     the line similar to  
     `3 packets transmitted, 3 received, 0% packet loss, time …`  
     For a healthy local loop-back the percentage **must be 0**.

2. Traceroute test  
   • Target host: 127.0.0.1  
   • Numeric output only (`-n`) so the hop list is fully predictable.  
   • A healthy result will show exactly one hop (the local machine itself).
     The hop-count is obtained by counting the lines that begin with an integer
     followed by a space in the main body of `traceroute`’s output; for
     127.0.0.1 that should be **1**.

3. Create the summary directory  
   • Full path: `/home/user/finops_diagnostics`  
   • Permissions: 755 (the default you get from `mkdir` with a standard umask
     is fine; do not use sudo).

4. Create the summary file  
   • Full path: `/home/user/finops_diagnostics/network_validation.log`  
   • The file **must contain exactly three lines, no blank lines, no extra
     whitespace**, and they must appear in the following order and format
     (the automated grader will compare them byte-for-byte):

     Line 1: `FINOPS_NETWORK_DIAGNOSTICS`  
     Line 2: `Ping_Packet_Loss: 0%`  
     Line 3: `Traceroute_Hops: 1`

     – `FINOPS_NETWORK_DIAGNOSTICS` is an all-caps literal marker.  
     – `Ping_Packet_Loss:` must be followed by a single space and the numeric
       percentage value **0%** (no decimal places).  
     – `Traceroute_Hops:` must be followed by a single space and the hop-count
       **1**.

5. Verification hints  
   • Your commands will resemble `ping -c 3 127.0.0.1` and
     `traceroute -n 127.0.0.1`, but decide yourself how to extract the numeric
     fields (grep + awk, cut, etc.).  
   • Make sure you overwrite any existing
     `/home/user/finops_diagnostics/network_validation.log`; the grader will
     look only at the final contents.  
   • After you have created the file you may `cat` it to the console to show the
     result, but that is optional.

A correctly completed task will therefore leave the system with the directory
`/home/user/finops_diagnostics/` containing a single file
`network_validation.log` whose three lines match exactly the specification
shown above.
truth:
Directory to look for:
  /home/user/finops_diagnostics   (must exist, 755)

File to look for:
  /home/user/finops_diagnostics/network_validation.log   (must exist, 644 or stricter)

Exact contents that the automated grader will check (no trailing spaces, LF line-endings):

Line 1: FINOPS_NETWORK_DIAGNOSTICS
Line 2: Ping_Packet_Loss: 0%
Line 3: Traceroute_Hops: 1
name: task_000000_067a8f39
